PanderThings live in a different economy from social media and smartphones — slower, more local, and a whole lot more fun on a proper screen. You can see most of this on your phone, but you'll be missing the best parts. Take it home and open it on a laptop, tablet or computer with a bit of time and no hurry.

In the meantime: share it in person. AirDrop it (iPhone) or Quick Share it (Android) to someone standing right next to you. That's exactly the kind of sharing this was made for.

If you'd like to lessen your fidgety phone dependency [all of us these days]… try our Bach Bees Playlist.

The Flying Pig

Coffee Shoppe  •  193 S. Main St., Russellville KY  •  flyingpigcoffee.com

A corner of kindness in a restored 1875 building on South Main Street. Tammy's pink door is always open — for a caffeine fix, a shoulder to cry on, or happiness in the form of carbs.

 •  A PanderThing from Thoth Industries / Prayer Group X, Lewisburg KY
01
A corner of kindness
The Flying Pig Coffee Shoppe

In a restored 1875 building on South Main Street in Russellville, KY, the Flying Pig Coffee Shoppe is a community-focused coffee house with a cozy country-chic atmosphere, a whimsical spiral staircase to a loft, and a pink striped door that is always, always open.

Owner Tammy Wellman took a leap of faith during the pandemic and purchased this beloved institution in 2021. She serves Bongo Java organic fair-trade coffee and milk from Chaney's Dairy. She feeds people. She hosts painters. She sends care packages to troops abroad.

The Flying Pig was nominated for Logan County's 2024 Small Business of the Year. There's a reason for that. 

Pharmanack — a fake Poor Richard's thingy
The Pink Door
after Poor Richard
He that opens a shop with a pink striped door invites the whole town in — and the whole town comes. For kindness is the oldest advertisement, and the best coffee is always the one made for a friend.
— Ibis, in the manner of Franklin

Not for social media. For people.

★ Locomprotion ★
Robyn's Nest Boutique

Right next door at 191 S. Main St. Curated women's fashion, accessories, and the real personal touch you only get when the owner is genuinely present.

191 S. Main St., Russellville KY • (270) 543-5127
02
Painting nights & free dinners
Arts & Community

Tammy hosts painting nights at the Flying Pig — evenings where people who've never touched a brush sit down, laugh a lot, and walk out with something beautiful. Logan County has been told for too long that it isn't interested in art. Tammy proves otherwise, quietly, every time.

She organises massive free community dinners for Halloween and Thanksgiving — for whoever shows up. She assembles care packages for military troops stationed abroad. She takes up causes that other people quietly step around, and brings love into all of it — not as performance, but as the actual operating principle of the place.

Many of us here don't give ourselves enough credit. Tammy proves us wrong with her loving yet smart attitude. 

Pharmanack — a fake Poor Richard's thingy
On Giving
after the Proverbs
The generous soul will be made rich, and he who waters will also be watered himself. Some people bring joy wherever they go; others bring joy whenever they go. Tammy brings joy wherever she goes, and the coffee is excellent.
— Ibis, after Proverbs 11:25

PanderThings travel by email, by hand, in parks and groceries.

★ Locomprotion ★
Robyn's Nest Boutique

Trendy and affordable women's fashion — dresses, tops, accessories, jewelry, purses. Shop local. Next door at 191 S. Main.

191 S. Main St., Russellville KY • (270) 543-5127
03
The woman behind the pink door
About Tammy

Tammy Wellman grew up in Ohio, came to Nashville at 13 to pursue country music, toured the country for years as Tammy Lando, and then fell in love, got married, and made some pretty adorable kiddos. She settled on a little horse farm in Russellville, earned a Business Management degree from WKU, and spent years managing multiple medical programs at Vanderbilt University.

One of her programs helped create the world's first artificial kidney. She doesn't lead with that. She leads with coffee and carbs and a genuinely open door. She's a single mom to three boys, outnumbered and not sad about it. The boys test mom's new recipes. You'll find them around the shop.

She's jumped out of planes. Climbed Mt. Fuji. Swum with sharks. When she saw the Flying Pig was for sale during the pandemic, she gasped — and then she bought it. "If God was going to send me a door, I'm pretty sure it would be pink striped." 

"Not much scares me. And somehow, buying a coffee shop is the scariest thing I've ever done."
Pharmanack — a fake Poor Richard's thingy
On Courage
in the manner of Ambrose Bierce
Courage: the quality of those who, having faced every large danger with composure, reserve their genuine terror for the truly important enterprises — such as the purchase of a pink-doored coffee shop during a global pandemic.
— Ibis, after the Devil's Dictionary

Please don't post this on social media. It was made for a different economy.

★ Locomprotion ★
Robyn's Nest Boutique

Robyn is a genuine woman — not afraid to share the real hardship of keeping a ma-and-pop shop open in the 2020s. Still standing. Still at 191 S. Main.

191 S. Main St., Russellville KY • (270) 543-5127
04
193 S. Main Street, Russellville KY
Come See Us

Find us at 193 S. Main Street, Russellville, KY 42276. Look for the pink striped door. You genuinely cannot miss it.

We serve Bongo Java organic fair-trade coffee and milk from Chaney's Dairy — local enough that you could wave at the cows. We bake. We cook. We host painting nights. We listen. We are usually open before you need us to be.

Visit us online at flyingpigcoffee.com and on Facebook. Better yet — come through the pink door. Tammy's probably there. 

The pink striped door is always open — be it a caffeine fix, a shoulder to cry on, or happiness in the form of carbs. You are all welcome here.
Pharmanack — a fake Poor Richard's thingy
On Hospitality
after Dr. Johnson
There is no private house in which people can enjoy themselves so well as at a good tavern. Let there be ever so much grandeur, ever so much elegance, ever so much desire — the pink door still beats the whole of it.
— Ibis, after Samuel Johnson

Some things are better in person. This is one of them.

Robyn's Nest Boutique

"Love Jesus, Shop Local, Be Kind." That's the vibe next door at 191 S. Main — and a regular presence at the downtown Sip and Shop nights.

05
What you're holding and why
The Star in the P

This file you're reading is a PanderThing. It's a self-contained HTML file — no app, no server, no tracking cookies, no algorithm. It knows nothing about you. Someone who thought you should know about the Flying Pig put it in your hands.

PanderThings are honest by design. The name is honest: it panders. But it panders to people, not to algorithms. It travels by email, by AirDrop, by Quick Share, hand to hand — in parks and groceries, between friends, across tables. It belongs to a social economy that predates the feed.

The star in the "P" is the SMET — the Social Media Exploitation Token. It means: we know exactly what this is, we're doing it anyway, and we're smiling about it. One toke over the line. 

PanderThings don't grow engagement. They assume you're already engaged — because someone you know handed it to you.
Pharmanack — a fake Poor Richard's thingy
The Medium
after Neil Postman
Every medium is a metaphor. The scroll says: keep moving, there is always more. The pink door says: come in. Sit down. There is nowhere better to be right now. We believe the pink door.
— Ibis, after Postman & the Flying Pig

Not for social media. For people.

★ Locomprotion ★
Robyn's Nest Boutique

Robyn works with regional artists and curates items with genuine personal taste. Check out what she has now — it'll be purdy, that's for sure.

191 S. Main St., Russellville KY • (270) 543-5127
06
Next door — 191 S. Main Street
Robyn's Nest Boutique

Right next door to the Flying Pig, at 191 S. Main Street, you'll find Robyn's Nest Boutique — curated women's fashion, accessories, jewelry, purses, and an intimate boutique atmosphere that only exists when the owner is genuinely present and genuinely cares.

Robyn has faced real challenges keeping a ma-and-pop business open in the 2020s, and she's still there. She's a key part of what makes this end of Main Street worth walking — a regular at the Sip and Shop nights that keep downtown Russellville alive.

She maintains an active social media presence with daily new arrivals and clearance announcements. She's the kind of neighbor that makes a block feel like a community. Go next door. Say hi. 

Pharmanack — a fake Poor Richard's thingy
On Neighbours
after Thoreau, mostly
I have a great deal of company in my house in the morning, when nobody calls. But on the street, next door, the door is also open. And that is better still.
— Ibis, after Walden, chapter on Visitors

The best economy is local. The best local economy is personal.
